---
title: "Feisty Upgrade - Geforce 420 Go - Failed to Load nVidia Kernel Module?"
date: 2007-05-10
forum: Hardware &amp; Laptops
---

### Post by th0r0n on 2007-05-10
**RESOLVED RESOLVED RESOLVED**
**Scroll to last post of mine**


Hi,

Just upgraded from Edgy to Feisty on my Toshiba 2410-S504 laptop.

I have a Geforce 420 Go! 32MB, and every time I try and enable my graphics card my X server fails to launch and I can't restart x until I cp my old xorg.conf.backup.

Anyone got any ideas? 

This is my Xorg.conf:

```
# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig

# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Mon Oct 16 22:13:07 PDT 2006



# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)

#

# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using

# values from the debconf database.

#

# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.

# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)

#

# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*

# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg

# package.

#

# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated

#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg



Section "ServerLayout"

    Identifier     "Default Layout"

    Screen         "Default Screen" 0 0

    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"

    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"

    InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"

EndSection

    Driver         "synaptics"

    Option         "SendCoreEvents" "true"
```

### Post by th0r0n on 2007-05-13
If you go into xorg.conf you should be able to change 'nvidia' to 'nv'

This should give you something working now, but 3D support won't be good.

I've tried loads of stuff, I get the Toshiba Black bar and a resolution of 800x600 but nothing higher.

I'm really totally at a loss now.

T

### Post by th0r0n on 2007-05-22
**RESOLVED**

Not correctly detecting the EDID on boot.

Went into NVIDIA-SETTINGS and dumped the EDID info (Can be found under DFP-0)

sudo apt-get install ghex 

When downloaded, run (Programming - Hex Editor) and go to "Edit - Go To" after OPENING edid.bin

Go to 56

Change to "00" (Mine was C9)

Go to 58

Change to "41" (Mine was 31)

Save in /etc/X11

Then in xorg.conf

Option "CustomEDID" "/etc/X11/edid.bin"

More here
[http://www.nvnews.net/vbulletin/showthread.php?t=84773](http://www.nvnews.net/vbulletin/showthread.php?t=84773)

I reckon this will fix most peoples problems without hacking the xorg file to bits.

Just make sure you have

Option "UseDisplayDevice" "DFP-0"

In your xorg.

I didn't need anything else.

Cheers,

T

---
